title: Add more visual effects and graphical enhancements to the 3D fighting game

requirements.summary: Add muzzle flash effect when UG shoots
acceptanceCriteria: A visible flash appears at UG's gun muzzle position each time a projectile is fired.
The flash is visually bright/emissive and disappears within a few frames.
No persistent visual artifact is left after the flash fades.

file_operations.description: Add a MuzzleFlash component that renders a bright emissive geometry (small flattened sphere or cross of planes) at the gun muzzle position. Track projectile creation events to trigger the flash, render it for 2-4 frames using a frame counter or time-based opacity fade, then remove it. Position the flash relative to UG's gun mesh position and ensure it uses emissive material with high intensity.

requirements.summary: Add explosion effect when projectile hits Sukuna
acceptanceCriteria: An explosion visual effect appears at the point of projectile impact on Sukuna.
The explosion uses fiery/cursed-energy colors consistent with the existing theme.
The effect scales up and fades out within approximately 0.4 seconds.
No persistent geometry remains after the explosion animation completes.

file_operations.description: Add an ExplosionEffect component that renders expanding emissive sphere/plane meshes with fiery colors (orange, red, gold). Listen for projectile hit events on Sukuna and spawn explosion instances at the hit position. Animate each explosion by scaling up and reducing opacity over 0.4 seconds, then remove the mesh. Use instanced meshes if multiple explosions can occur simultaneously.

Extend the particle system to support explosion particle bursts. Add a new particle type or effect mode that emits a radial burst of particles with fiery colors at a given position. Configure particles to scale up and fade out over approximately 0.4 seconds to match the explosion effect timing.

requirements.summary: Add persistent cursed-energy aura effect around Sukuna
acceptanceCriteria: Sukuna has a visible pulsing aura effect rendered around their mesh at all times during battle.
The aura uses deep crimson or dark magenta emissive coloring.
The aura subtly scales or pulses in a looping animation rather than remaining static.
The effect does not obscure Sukuna's character mesh.

file_operations.description: Add a CursedEnergyAura component that renders a semi-transparent emissive mesh (enlarged sphere or distorted shell) around Sukuna's position. Use deep crimson/dark magenta color with emissive material. Animate the aura scale using a sine wave or easing function to pulse between 0.95 and 1.05 scale over 2-3 seconds in a loop. Position the aura to follow Sukuna's mesh and ensure it renders behind the character (adjust renderOrder or use transparency blending).

requirements.summary: Add ground impact shockwave effect when characters land from jump
acceptanceCriteria: A shockwave disc appears on the ground at the character's landing position when they land from a jump.
The disc expands radially and fades out over approximately 0.3 seconds.
UG's shockwave uses blue/cool coloring; Sukuna's uses red/warm coloring.
No shockwave is shown when the character is simply walking (only on jump landing).

file_operations.description: Add a ShockwaveEffect component that renders a flat disc mesh on the ground plane. Detect landing events by checking when a character's velocityY transitions from negative to zero/positive while grounded. Spawn a shockwave instance at the landing position with team-colored material (blue for UG, red for Sukuna). Animate the disc by scaling its radius from near-zero to 2-3 units and fading opacity from 1 to 0 over 0.3 seconds, then remove the mesh.

Add landing detection logic to track when player and enemy transition from airborne to grounded state. Pass landing events to Scene3D via a callback or state update so shockwave effects can be triggered at the correct position and time. Ensure landing is only detected after a jump, not during normal walking.

acceptanceCriteria: Traveling projectiles have a visible trailing particle or ribbon effect behind them.
Trail uses electric gold or cyan emissive coloring.
Trail particles fade quickly so they don't linger beyond 0.2 seconds after the projectile has passed.
The trail does not appear for melee attacks, only for gun projectiles.

file_operations.description: Add a ProjectileTrail component that renders a stream of small emissive particles or a tapered ribbon mesh behind each gun projectile. As projectiles move, emit trail particles at regular intervals (every frame or every few frames) at the projectile's current position. Use electric gold/cyan emissive material and fade particles over 0.2 seconds. Alternatively, use a ribbon/trail mesh that stretches from the projectile's current position to its previous positions, fading the oldest segments.

Extend the particle system to support trail particles. Add a method to spawn trail particles at a given position with a short lifetime (0.2 seconds). Configure trail particles to use electric gold or cyan colors and fade out quickly. Ensure trail particles are visually distinct from impact particles.

requirements.summary: Add dynamic screen-edge vignette flash overlay when UG takes damage
acceptanceCriteria: A red semi-transparent vignette/border flash is visible around the screen edges when UG takes a hit.
The flash fades out within approximately 0.5 seconds.
The effect does not block game controls or obscure health bars.
The effect triggers only when UG loses health, not when Sukuna takes damage.

file_operations.description: Add state to track when UG takes damage and trigger a damage flash effect. Detect when player health decreases and set a flash trigger timestamp. Render a DOM overlay (absolutely positioned div covering the viewport) with a radial gradient vignette effect in red. Animate the overlay's opacity from 0.6 to 0 over 0.5 seconds using CSS transitions or requestAnimationFrame. Ensure the overlay has pointer-events: none so it doesn't block controls.

If the damage flash overlay is implemented as a separate component, wire it into App.tsx to receive damage events from FightingGame. Alternatively, ensure the overlay is rendered within FightingGame itself and does not interfere with the header or footer layout.
